# JP Digital Works — Gold-Standard Voice Samples
*Generated by brand-voice skill — 2026-03-29*

These sentences represent the voice at its most authentic.
Use for reference, training, or comparison when output feels off.

---

## Built sample — Email opening
"You didn't start a plumbing business to spend three hours a week posting on Facebook. Let's automate that so you can get back to actual work."
*Why it's representative: Leads with empathy for the reader's situation, names a specific industry, and ends with a clear benefit — all in two sentences with zero jargon.*

## Built sample — LinkedIn post hook
"Your competitors with bigger budgets? They're not smarter — they just automated first. The gap is closable, and it's cheaper than you think."
*Why it's representative: Bold, direct, addresses the David vs. Goliath pain that local business owners feel. Ends with reassurance, not a hard sell.*

## Built sample — Landing page hero
"The reality is, you're losing leads right now because nobody answers the phone at 9pm. An AI assistant handles that for you — every call, every time."
*Why it's representative: Uses the signature "the reality is" phrase, names a specific revenue problem, and presents a concrete solution without tech jargon.*

## Built sample — Social post
"I just set up an AI system for a landscaping company that handles every quote request, follow-up, and review ask — automatically. Owner got 6 hours back last week. That's a full day of actual work."
*Why it's representative: Specific industry, specific result, grounded in real numbers. Doesn't say "AI-powered solution" — says what it actually does.*

## Built sample — Ad copy
"Still doing invoices by hand? Your HVAC competitor down the road automated that two months ago. He's not working harder — he's working with AI."
*Why it's representative: Starts with a relatable pain, uses the competitor angle for urgency, and keeps the language conversational and industry-specific.*

## Built sample — Email closing
"Here's what I'd do if I were you: pick the one task you hate the most. The one that eats your Tuesday afternoons. Let's automate that first. Everything else gets easier after."
*Why it's representative: Empathetic and practical — gives a concrete first step instead of a vague CTA. Sounds like advice from someone who gets it.*

## Built sample — Objection handling
"I get it — you've been burned by marketing people before. Paid for a website that didn't bring in calls. Ran ads that went nowhere. This is different because you'll see it working in the first week, not the first quarter."
*Why it's representative: Validates past frustration (empathy), then pivots to a specific, time-bound claim (direct). Addresses skepticism head-on without being defensive.*
